---
title: "OutfitDB"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 2
    number-sections: false
    page-layout: article
    smooth-scroll: true
---

```{=html}
<style>
.od-hero {
  text-align: center;
  padding: 3rem 1rem 2rem;
  border-bottom: 1px solid #eee;
  margin-bottom: 2.5rem;
}
.od-hero h1 {
  font-size: 3rem;
  font-weight: 800;
  margin: 0 0 0.4rem;
  letter-spacing: -0.5px;
  color: #111;
}
.od-hero .od-tag {
  font-size: 1rem;
  color: #888;
  letter-spacing: 0.4px;
  margin: 0 auto 1.6rem;
  max-width: 640px;
  line-height: 1.6;
}
.od-hero .od-version {
  display: inline-block;
  background: #f6f6f4;
  color: #555;
  padding: 3px 10px;
  border-radius: 4px;
  font-size: 0.78rem;
  font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
  margin-left: 6px;
  vertical-align: middle;
}
.od-download-row {
  display: flex; gap: 14px; flex-wrap: wrap; justify-content: center;
  margin-top: 1.6rem;
}
.od-dl-btn {
  display: inline-block;
  padding: 14px 28px;
  background: #111;
  color: #fff !important;
  text-decoration: none;
  border-radius: 10px;
  font-weight: 600;
  font-size: 1rem;
  transition: transform 0.1s, box-shadow 0.15s;
  min-width: 220px;
}
.od-dl-btn:hover {
  transform: translateY(-2px);
  box-shadow: 0 6px 16px rgba(0,0,0,0.18);
}
.od-dl-btn.secondary {
  background: #fff;
  color: #111 !important;
  border: 1px solid #ccc;
}
.od-dl-btn .od-dl-os {
  display: block;
  font-size: 0.7rem;
  color: #aaa;
  letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  margin-bottom: 4px;
}
.od-dl-btn.secondary .od-dl-os { color: #999; }
.od-quick-stats {
  display: grid;
  /* 4-up desktop · 2-up mobile so 8 cards always tile cleanly into
     2 rows × 4 cols (or 4 rows × 2 cols), no awkward 5+3 wraps */
  grid-template-columns: repeat(4, 1fr);
  gap: 14px;
  margin: 2rem 0 3rem;
}
@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .od-quick-stats { grid-template-columns: repeat(2, 1fr); }
}
.od-stat {
  background: #fafaf8;
  padding: 18px 14px;
  border-radius: 10px;
  text-align: center;
  border: 1px solid #eee;
}
.od-stat-val {
  font-size: 1.7rem;
  font-weight: 700;
  color: #111;
  line-height: 1.1;
}
.od-stat-val small { font-size: 0.65em; color: #888; font-weight: 500; }
.od-stat-label {
  font-size: 0.75rem;
  color: #777;
  letter-spacing: 0.4px;
  text-transform: uppercase;
  margin-top: 6px;
}
.od-feature {
  display: grid;
  grid-template-columns: 1fr 1fr;
  gap: 22px;
  margin: 1.6rem 0;
}
.od-feature-card {
  background: #fff;
  border: 1px solid #ececec;
  border-radius: 10px;
  padding: 18px 20px;
}
.od-feature-card h3 {
  margin: 0 0 0.4rem;
  font-size: 1.05rem;
  color: #111;
}
.od-feature-card p {
  font-size: 0.92rem;
  line-height: 1.55;
  color: #555;
  margin: 0;
}
@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .od-feature { grid-template-columns: 1fr; }
  .od-hero h1 { font-size: 2.2rem; }
}
.od-install-note {
  background: #fff7ec;
  border: 1px solid #f0d590;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 12px 16px;
  margin: 1rem 0;
  font-size: 0.92rem;
  color: #6b4400;
}
.od-install-note code {
  background: rgba(0,0,0,0.05);
  padding: 1px 5px;
  border-radius: 3px;
}
.od-pipeline {
  display: flex;
  align-items: stretch;
  gap: 8px;
  flex-wrap: wrap;
  background: #fafaf8;
  padding: 18px;
  border-radius: 10px;
  border: 1px solid #eee;
  margin: 1rem 0;
}
.od-pipeline-stage {
  flex: 1; min-width: 160px;
  background: #fff;
  border: 1px solid #ddd;
  border-radius: 8px;
  padding: 12px 14px;
}
.od-pipeline-stage .stage-no {
  font-size: 0.7rem; color: #999; letter-spacing: 0.5px;
  text-transform: uppercase; font-weight: 600;
}
.od-pipeline-stage .stage-name {
  font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 700; color: #111; margin: 4px 0;
}
.od-pipeline-stage .stage-desc {
  font-size: 0.82rem; color: #666; line-height: 1.45;
}
.od-pipeline-mult {
  align-self: center;
  font-size: 1.4rem; color: #aaa; font-weight: 700;
}
@media (max-width: 700px) {
  .od-pipeline-mult { display: none; }
}
.od-stack {
  display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 8px;
  margin: 0.8rem 0 1.4rem;
}
.od-stack span {
  background: #f6f6f4; color: #444;
  padding: 5px 11px; border-radius: 5px;
  font-size: 0.84rem;
  font-family: ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, Menlo, Consolas, monospace;
}
</style>
```

```{=html}
<div class="od-hero">
  <h1>OutfitDB <span class="od-version">v0.2.0</span></h1>
  <p class="od-tag">
    OutfitDB shows how anyone can build a personal database and train
    their own ML model for everyday problems that don&rsquo;t have a
    tidy mathematical answer. Three short rating flows teach it your
    sense of temperature, your aesthetic, and your sense of occasion
    &mdash; and the recommendations even surface combinations from your
    own closet you&rsquo;d never have thought of yourself. Local-first:
    your data never leaves your machine.
  </p>
  <div class="od-download-row">
    <a class="od-dl-btn" href="https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB/releases/download/v0.2.0/OutfitDB-0.2.0.dmg">
      <span class="od-dl-os">macOS &middot; 95 MB</span>
      Download for Mac
    </a>
    <a class="od-dl-btn" href="https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB/releases/download/v0.2.0/OutfitDB-0.2.0-windows.zip">
      <span class="od-dl-os">Windows &middot; 200 MB</span>
      Download for Windows
    </a>
  </div>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div class="od-quick-stats">
  <!-- Row 1: emotional / benefit hook -->
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">1<small>-of-</small>1</div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Built for one. You.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">Tailored</div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Designed around your real life</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">Discovery</div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Combos from your own closet you&rsquo;d never try</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">30<small> sec</small></div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">From &ldquo;nothing to wear&rdquo; to ranked outfits</div>
  </div>
  <!-- Row 2: technical credibility / privacy -->
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">11</div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Tables in your own database</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">3<small> axes</small></div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Train weather, taste, occasion separately</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">Live</div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Wardrobe state in real time</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-stat">
    <div class="od-stat-val">0<small> cloud</small></div>
    <div class="od-stat-label">Nothing leaves your machine</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Live demo

The full FastAPI backend, deployed to a Render free instance and pre-
seeded with the *Tester* wardrobe (54 items, 17&thinsp;MB of photos).
Every interaction hits the real Python service: SQLAlchemy talks to
SQLite, XGBoost ranks outfits, Jinja renders templates, the lifespan
hook copies the bundled seed into `/tmp` on each cold boot. The
recommendation flow, profile switcher, training pages, calendar,
context builder, and laundry/dirty/wear state machine all run end-to-
end &mdash; this isn&rsquo;t a static mock anymore.

```{=html}
<link rel="preconnect" href="https://outfitdb.onrender.com">
<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://outfitdb.onrender.com">

<div style="border:1px solid #ddd;border-radius:10px;overflow:hidden;
            box-shadow:0 2px 12px rgba(0,0,0,0.05);margin:18px 0;">
  <iframe src="https://outfitdb.onrender.com/"
          width="100%" height="860"
          style="display:block;border:0;background:#f6f6f4;"
          loading="lazy"
          allow="geolocation"
          title="OutfitDB live demo">
  </iframe>
</div>

<p style="font-size:0.85rem;color:#777;margin:-6px 0 18px;">
  Render&rsquo;s free tier sleeps the instance after 15 minutes of
  inactivity, so the very first visit can take ~50 seconds while the
  container wakes up. Subsequent loads are instant. If the iframe
  doesn&rsquo;t respond, open
  <a href="https://outfitdb.onrender.com/" target="_blank"
     style="color:#666;">the demo URL</a> directly &mdash; the wake-up
  takes the same time either way.
  Each cold boot wipes <code>/tmp</code> and re-seeds Tester from the
  bundled snapshot, so the demo always returns to a clean state.
</p>
```

## What it does

You upload photos of your clothes once. The app learns three independent
axes of preference from your ratings, then chains them at recommendation
time so a single hard fail (wrong temperature, wrong occasion) drives the
score to zero regardless of how aesthetically pleasing the outfit is.

```{=html}
<div class="od-pipeline">
  <div class="od-pipeline-stage">
    <div class="stage-no">Stage 1</div>
    <div class="stage-name">Temperature</div>
    <div class="stage-desc">Six zones (subzero / cold / cool / mild / warm / hot). Multi-label classifier — one outfit can suit multiple zones.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-pipeline-mult">&times;</div>
  <div class="od-pipeline-stage">
    <div class="stage-no">Stage 2</div>
    <div class="stage-name">Aesthetic</div>
    <div class="stage-desc">Pure look rating: color, cut, layering. User scores on a four-point scale ignoring temperature and occasion.</div>
  </div>
  <div class="od-pipeline-mult">&times;</div>
  <div class="od-pipeline-stage">
    <div class="stage-no">Stage 3</div>
    <div class="stage-name">Occasion</div>
    <div class="stage-desc">Nine event types from gym to formal_event. Multi-label so one outfit can be wearable for multiple events.</div>
  </div>
</div>
```

The recommendation score multiplies all three signals with two soft
factors (freshness, recovery) so the model both surfaces neglected items
and respects pure-axis judgments.

## Why local-first

The data the app collects &mdash; what you wear, what you don&rsquo;t,
what you rated &ldquo;love&rdquo; vs &ldquo;hate&rdquo; &mdash; is
unusually personal. Hosting that on a shared server creates obligations
(authentication, encryption at rest, GDPR-style data handling) that
undermine the user&rsquo;s trust without buying anything they need.

OutfitDB ships as a desktop app. Each user&rsquo;s data lives in their
own OS-standard application data directory
(`~/Library/Application Support/OutfitDB/profiles/{name}/` on macOS,
`%APPDATA%/OutfitDB/profiles/{name}/` on Windows), inside a single
SQLite file plus an images folder. The app launches uvicorn bound only
to `127.0.0.1`, opens the system browser, and runs entirely on the
user&rsquo;s machine. Nothing crosses the network except the optional
weather and geocoding lookups (both calls go from the user&rsquo;s
browser directly to the public APIs, never through any server I run).

## Six implementation phases

```{=html}
<div class="od-feature">
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 1 &mdash; Layer Coverage Model</h3>
    <p>Thermal coverage formula <code>T_cover = T_BASE &minus; &alpha;&middot;coverage</code> with dual curves (comfort + aesthetic) determines optimal layer count for any temperature. Replaces hand-coded weather rules.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 2 &mdash; XGBoost Preference</h3>
    <p>Per-user binary classifier on a 100-dimensional feature vector. Cold-start falls back to mean item rating; warm model swaps in once 10+ ratings exist.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 3 &mdash; Local-First Storage</h3>
    <p>SQLite per-profile schema with idempotent ALTER TABLE migrations. Profile folders survive app updates because they live outside the bundle.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 4 &mdash; Multi-Stage Training</h3>
    <p>Pure-axis labeling: three independent classifiers trained on disjoint rating tables. Inference multiplies the three probabilities so one bad axis kills the score.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 5 &mdash; Discovery Polish</h3>
    <p>Fabric-blend composition (40/60 cotton/linen as weighted multi-hot), four-state lifecycle (clean / worn / in_laundry / unavailable), new-item priority training, force-anyway override for unusual outfits.</p>
  </div>
  <div class="od-feature-card">
    <h3>Phase 6 &mdash; Desktop Packaging</h3>
    <p>PyInstaller .spec handles the FastAPI-stack frozen-mode gotchas (Jinja2 paths, PIL plugins, xgboost native lib). Auto-update via cached public manifest. macOS DMG with drag-to-Applications layout.</p>
  </div>
</div>
```

## Tech stack

```{=html}
<div class="od-stack">
  <span>FastAPI</span>
  <span>SQLAlchemy 2.x</span>
  <span>SQLite</span>
  <span>XGBoost</span>
  <span>NumPy</span>
  <span>Pillow</span>
  <span>Pydantic v2</span>
  <span>Jinja2</span>
  <span>vanilla JS</span>
  <span>Service Worker</span>
  <span>PyInstaller</span>
</div>
```

## macOS

1.  Click **Download for Mac** above. You&rsquo;ll get
    `OutfitDB-0.2.0.dmg`.
2.  Double-click the DMG. A window opens showing **OutfitDB.app** next
    to an **Applications** folder shortcut.
3.  Drag **OutfitDB.app** onto **Applications**. Installed.
4.  Launch via Spotlight (`&#8984; Space` &rarr; type "OutfitDB"),
    Launchpad, or Finder &rarr; Applications.

```{=html}
<div class="od-install-note">
<b>First launch only:</b> macOS Gatekeeper blocks unsigned apps with
&ldquo;developer cannot be verified.&rdquo; <b>Right-click the app
&rarr; Open &rarr; confirm.</b> Subsequent launches work normally.
This will go away once the app is code-signed in the future.
</div>
```

On first launch the app **automatically seeds a `Tester` profile** with
54 sample items (the same wardrobe used in the live demo above) so you
can immediately click around the recommendation flow without having to
upload your own clothes first. Add your own profile any time via the
top-right user switcher; deleting `Tester` won&rsquo;t bring it back on
next launch (the seed only runs when the profile folder is empty). The
app loads in your default browser at `http://127.0.0.1:18888`.

## Windows {#windows}

Grab the **`OutfitDB-0.2.0-windows.zip`** asset from the
[**v0.2.0 release**](https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB/releases/tag/v0.2.0)
(or use the **Download for Windows** button at the top of this page).
Unzip anywhere &mdash; e.g., `C:\Users\<you>\Apps\OutfitDB\` &mdash;
and double-click **`OutfitDB.exe`**. The app starts a local web
server on `http://127.0.0.1:18888` and opens it in your default
browser.

```{=html}
<div class="od-install-note">
<b>First launch only:</b> Windows SmartScreen blocks unsigned apps with
&ldquo;Microsoft Defender SmartScreen prevented an unrecognized app
from starting.&rdquo; Click <b>More info &rarr; Run anyway.</b>
Subsequent launches work normally. This will go away once the binary
is code-signed in the future.
</div>
```

The Windows build is produced by a GitHub Actions workflow
([build-windows.yml](https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB/blob/main/.github/workflows/build-windows.yml))
that runs `pyinstaller outfitdb.spec` on a `windows-latest` runner
every time a `v*` tag is pushed to the source repo. Same wardrobe
seeding flow as macOS &mdash; first launch auto-creates the bundled
*Tester* profile.

If you&rsquo;d rather build from source:

```bash
# Windows + Python 3.11 / 3.12
git clone https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB.git
cd OutfitDB
python -m venv .venv
.venv\Scripts\activate
pip install -r requirements.txt
pip install pyinstaller
pyinstaller outfitdb.spec --noconfirm
# Output: dist\OutfitDB\OutfitDB.exe
```

## Database design

The full entity-relationship model, schema rationale, and migration
strategy are documented in the
[**EPPS&nbsp;6354 Information Management Final Report &rarr;**](EPPS6354.html#final-report).
That page is the formal academic deliverable for the course; this page
is the end-user-facing landing page for the application itself.

## Source &amp; documentation

Source is on GitHub at
[**chentzuyuan/OutfitDB**](https://github.com/chentzuyuan/OutfitDB).
A formal technical writeup of the system &mdash; schema, three key design
decisions, ML pipeline, and deployment &mdash; is available as a PDF and
as a web section:

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 14px 0 6px; display: flex; flex-wrap: wrap; gap: 12px;">
  <a href="chen_finalreport.pdf" target="_blank" rel="noopener"
     style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
            flex: 1 1 240px; min-height: 56px; padding: 14px 22px;
            border-radius: 10px; background: #111; color: #fff !important;
            font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none !important;">
    Final Report PDF &rarr;
  </a>
  <a href="EPPS6354.html#final-report"
     style="display: inline-flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;
            flex: 1 1 240px; min-height: 56px; padding: 14px 22px;
            border-radius: 10px; background: #f6f6f4; color: #111 !important;
            border: 1px solid #ddd; font-weight: 600; text-decoration: none !important;">
    Web version &rarr;
  </a>
</div>
```

```{=html}
<div style="margin: 2.5rem 0 3rem; text-align: center;">
  <a href="index.html" style="color: #888; text-decoration: none; font-size: 0.92rem;">&larr; Back to home</a>
</div>
```
